channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Moving from NYC to North Carolina released the creativity for Snail Mail's new album
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Lindsey Jordan of Snail Mail about their new album, Ricochet.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Lindsey Jordan of Snail Mail about their new album, Ricochet.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Scott

item.title: For years, scientists could only prove that DOGS existed for 10,000 years — until now
item.description: A new study genetically shows dogs existed almost 5,000 years earlier than scientists could prove.
item.content:encoded: A new study genetically shows dogs existed almost 5,000 years earlier than scientists could prove.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Chinese artist who satirized Mao to stand trial for 'slandering heroes and martyrs'
item.description: Gao Zhen, a famed contemporary artist, goes on trial this week for "slandering heroes and martyrs" of China's ruling Communist Party. His wife has been forced to become his advocate.
item.content:encoded: Gao Zhen, a famed contemporary artist, goes on trial this week for "slandering heroes and martyrs" of China's ruling Communist Party. His wife has been forced to become his advocate.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Epstein survivor responds to lawsuit over release of identifying information
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Epstein survivor Danielle Bensky about a new class action lawsuit against the Justice Department and Google over the release of identifying information about victims.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Epstein survivor Danielle Bensky about a new class action lawsuit against the Justice Department and Google over the release of identifying information about victims.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: Archaeologists may have found the remains Three Musketeers hero d'Artagnan
item.description: Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers.
item.content:encoded: Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Fear and exhaustion in Lebanon as Israel broadens invasion
item.description: First, Israel said it would take Lebanese territory up to the Litani River. Then it ordered people out of an area 10 miles north of that. Residents are fleeing southern Lebanon in waves.
item.content:encoded: First, Israel said it would take Lebanese territory up to the Litani River. Then it ordered people out of an area 10 miles north of that. Residents are fleeing southern Lebanon in waves.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Homesick in a foreign country, a teenager meets a lifelong friend
item.description: Holly Deiaco-Smith was feeling homesick while studying abroad in France when she was 19 years old. An encounter at the post office changed everything and led to a decades-long friendship.
item.content:encoded: Holly Deiaco-Smith was feeling homesick while studying abroad in France when she was 19 years old. An encounter at the post office changed everything and led to a decades-long friendship.

item.title: Farmworker communities are still reeling after Chavez sexual abuse allegations
item.description: Cesar Chavez once lived in Delano, home of the United Farm Workers' first HQ. In the wake of sexual abuse allegations against him, many in the city struggle over removing his name from public spaces.
item.content:encoded: Cesar Chavez once lived in Delano, home of the United Farm Workers' first HQ. In the wake of sexual abuse allegations against him, many in the city struggle over removing his name from public spaces.
item.dc:creator: Kerry Klein

item.title: Meet 81-year-old Bonnie Shea, a trailblazer for U.S. women's hockey
item.description: In 1951, Bonnie Shea was the only girl in Duluth, Minnesota playing organized hockey. But when she got to high school, she couldn't play on the boys' team. Now, at age 81, Shea is still competing.
item.content:encoded: In 1951, Bonnie Shea was the only girl in Duluth, Minnesota playing organized hockey. But when she got to high school, she couldn't play on the boys' team. Now, at age 81, Shea is still competing.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Kraker

item.title: Is the U.K. 'Saturday Night Live' working? Viewers are 'pleasantly surprised'
item.description: Two shows in, Saturday Night Live UK viewers are pleasantly surprised by what they're seeing. The late night spinoff has a lot to prove in a short season.
item.content:encoded: Two shows in, Saturday Night Live UK viewers are pleasantly surprised by what they're seeing. The late night spinoff has a lot to prove in a short season.
item.dc:creator: Ilya Marritz
